children.text: The work shifts difficulty from raw stats to readable decisions.
Layer-aware Spikes exists, but no dedicated active revealed-Rapid-Spin penalty was found in the Spikes branch.
Stack only when it still converts; otherwise pressure or cash out against the revealed spinner.
Existing pressure checks reduce bad setup, but the plan calls for tighter low-HP public-KO cases.
Setup is suppressed unless it immediately wins, survives, or preserves the route.
Self-KO moves already have risk handling, but route-value discipline is a later target.
Explosion-style trades fire when they remove a public route piece, not for low-value chip.
Weighted best-vs-second already exists; broad new randomness is not the first patch.
More close choices become human-plausible only after hard rejects keep bad moves out.
